comments.body: @ipbabble Remove your original, it is already merged, and just submit the second patch.
Ha! I knew I didn't need to git add. I should have just done a git checkout and push, no?

Just do a 
git pull upstream master
git rebase -i origin
Remove the first patch if it is still there.
save
git push --force
Hi Dan those instructions didn't work for me. Sigh

What do you call upstream?  Origin?

git rebase -i origin

$ git remote -v
origin	git@github.com:ipbabble/buildah.git (fetch)
origin	git@github.com:ipbabble/buildah.git (push)
upstream	https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah.git (fetch)
upstream	https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah.git (push)

$ git branch
  master
* tutorial-fix
  tutorials

Oh yeah joy. Should just take me a couple of days to figure out the correct
git spell sequence.

Git alohomora Or git anapneo. ?? Harry Potter fans help me with git.

On Tue, Oct 31, 2017 at 5:13 PM Tom Sweeney <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> *@TomSweeneyRedHat* commented on this pull request.
> ------------------------------
>
> In docs/tutorials/01-intro.md
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/304#discussion_r148151325>:
>
> > +
> +We can inspect the container's metadata using the `inspect` command:
> +
> +    buildah inspect $newcontainer
> +
> +We should probably unmount and commit the image:
> +
> +     buildah unmount $newcontainer
> +     buildah commit $newcontainer fedora-bashecho
> +     buildah images
> +
> +And you can see there is a new image called `fedora-bashecho:latest`. You can inspect the new image using:
> +
> +    buildah inspect --type=image fedora-bashecho
> +
> +Later when you want to create a new container or containers from this image, you simply need need to do `buildah from fedora-bashecho`. This will create a duplicate copy of this image for you.
>
> buildah from creates a container from the image, not an image. So I think
> you want the last sentence here to say:
>
> "This will create a new container based on this image for you." or
> something to that effect.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
>
>
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/304#pullrequestreview-73350718>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/ABPpgcHM_Yn9G7P5_9ajhH_bG_BP9ofKks5sx6mmgaJpZM4QNMUU>
> .

-- 
Sent from Gmail Mobile on iPhone

Never mind. Context. Got it

>
>  ## Using Dockerfiles with Buildah
>
> -What if you have been using docker for a while and have some existing Dockerfiles. Not a problem. `buildah` can build images using a Dockerfile. The `build-using-dockerfile`, or `bud` for short, takes a Dockefile as input and produces an OCI image.
> +What if you have been using Docker for a while and have some existing Dockerfiles. Not a problem. Buildah can build images using a Dockerfile. The `build-using-dockerfile`, or `bud` for short, takes a Dockefile as input and produces an OCI image.
>
> "Dockefile as input" to "Dockerfile as input"

@rhatdan some CI thing failing.

CI isn't an issue with this submit.  It looks to be some kind of issue in container/storage?

```
HEAD is now at c5ec2548... Merge pull request #1527 from mrunalp/spec_bump_v1
# ln -sf ../../opencontainers/runc/runc
# github.com/projectatomic/buildah/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/pkg/devicemapper
vendor/github.com/containers/storage/pkg/devicemapper/devmapper_wrapper_deferred_remove.go:12:13: could not determine kind of name for C.dm_task_deferred_remove
# github.com/projectatomic/buildah/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/drivers/btrfs
vendor/github.com/containers/storage/drivers/btrfs/version.go:6:27: fatal error: btrfs/version.h: No such file or directory

 #include <btrfs/version.h>
~~~
Between lines 847 and 867 of that build log, I'm guessing something's not right with the Go tip.

Yes Tom. I thought about that prompt idea myself. I had decided to include
it if there might be some output or if it was inside the container.

I really didn't like the '#' (root) prompt showing up in the examples when
it might change later to '$'



On Wed, Nov 1, 2017 at 11:35 AM, Tom Sweeney <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> @ipbabble <https://github.com/ipbabble>. Looks really good. One last
> thing to drive you crazy with. In your examples in some places you have a
> command prompt, and others you do not. I think you probably ought to have
> them in all, especially the ones where you've a mixture of running
> something and it's output (i.e. the docker container running sample).
> Probably worth ask @jberkus <https://github.com/jberkus> about for a
> point of sanity.
>
> FWIW, I ran through the tutorial again on a new vm, it ran really nicely.
>
> Will look at the CI fun.
>

@TomSweeneyRedHat @cgwalters any idea about this Go tip failure?

@ipbabble As far as the prompts go, that's fine, but I'd still like to see one added at line 158 for this one:
docker run fedora-bashecho 

As it has a number of output lines following.  The corresponding buildah example has the prompt.  But a really small nit, we can touch it up later.

As far as the tip issue goes, I've a thought, but haven't tried it yet.  Regardless this PR is just documentation based and is not the cause of the build issue.  @rhatdan @nalind I'm fine with merging at your convenience.
This didn't merge.
#307 replaces this submit.  Let's keep this one closed.
